_meta.purpose: Legality of volume / quantity discounts to the trade by jurisdiction, to drive the 'where legal' flag in the pricing walk and strategy.
_meta.disclaimer: GENERAL GUIDANCE, NOT LEGAL ADVICE. US alcohol pricing law is state-specific, changes often, and is enforced by each state's ABC. Always confirm against the current state statute / regulation or qualified counsel before recommending a discount structure. Categories below are simplifications.

categories.open: Pricing relatively free; quantity discounts to retailers generally permitted unless otherwise noted.
categories.post-and-hold: Wholesalers must file (post) prices with the state and hold them for a set period; discounts must be posted and offered to all on equal terms within a price tier.
categories.franchise: Supplier-distributor relationship is statutorily protected (hard to terminate); affects deal structure more than per-unit discounting.
categories.control: The state itself is the wholesaler and/or retailer; private quantity discounting to retail does not apply in the usual sense.

US.federalNote: Federal FAA Act 'tied house' and 'quantity discount' rules restrict inducements; many states layer stricter rules. Quantity discounts that are not available to all retailers on equal terms are a common violation.

defaultIfStateUnlisted.note: State not in table — confirm with the state ABC before assuming.

NY.note: NY ABC bans volume/quantity discounts to retailers; wholesalers post monthly prices held for the month. Discounts must be in posted schedule and uniform.

CA.note: Relatively liberal; quantity discounts generally permitted but must avoid tied-house inducements.

NJ.note: Discounts must be offered to all licensees of the same class on equal terms.

TX.note: TABC rules on discounts and dual-tier ownership; confirm.

MA.note: Volume discounts to retailers restricted; price postings apply.

PA.note: PLCB is the wholesaler/retailer; engage the board's listing process.

MI.note: Control state for spirits; wine rules differ — confirm.

UK.note: No three-tier system. Volume discounts are normal commercial practice. Watch the duty system, minimum unit pricing in Scotland/Wales (off-trade), and competition-law constraints on RPM (resale price maintenance).

FR.note: Quantity discounts are common, but resale below cost (revente a perte) is prohibited and commercial terms are framed by the Code de commerce. Loi Evin constrains alcohol MARKETING, not pricing. Confirm CGV (conditions generales de vente).

CA.note: Provincial boards set retail prices; private quantity discounting to retail does not apply. Trade pricing is via the board's quote/listing and any licensee/agency programmes.
